title: Caramelized Banana Milkshake
imagePrompt: A tall glass holds a thick, warm-ivory milkshake with a faint golden-caramel tint throughout. A generous cloud of whipped cream crowns the top, its surface cracked and amber where the brown sugar has been torched into a thin brûléed crust. Dark caramel sauce drizzles down through the whipped cream and traces lazy streaks along the inside of the glass.
subtitle: Bananas Foster-style caramelized banana blended into a vanilla milkshake. Whipped cream, caramel, torched sugar on top.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Inspired by @cookingwithcarrrl. Riffs on Bananas Foster — butter, brown sugar, ripe banana.
items: 1 ripe banana
1 tbsp butter
2 tbsp brown sugar (plus extra for the top)
¼ cup milk
½ cup vanilla ice cream
Whipped cream (for topping)
Caramel sauce (for topping)
ingredients.callout: Banana ripeness matters: Spotty, ripe bananas caramelize best — more sugar to work with. Green bananas stay starchy and don't soften right.

body.html: Pan-caramelize a sliced banana in butter and brown sugar — Bananas Foster style — then blend it hot into milk and vanilla ice cream for a deep, toasty banana milkshake. Top with whipped cream, caramel, and (optionally) torched brown sugar.

items: Slice + caramelize banana in butter and brown sugar
Baste the second side in the bubbling sauce
Blend with milk, then add ice cream
Top with whipped cream + caramel (torch optional)

body.html: Whole thing takes ~6–8 minutes. The slowest step is caramelizing the banana — everything after that is a quick blend.

items: Heat a skillet over medium heat. Melt 1 tbsp butter.
Slice the banana lengthwise and add to the skillet, cut side down.
Sprinkle 2 tbsp brown sugar over the top.
Cook ~3 minutes until the bottom is deep golden.
Flip the banana → baste in the bubbling sauce for ~2 more minutes.

body.html: You want real color. Pale = no flavor. Push it until the sugar turns into a glossy, mahogany caramel sauce in the pan.

slides.title: Blend with Milk
slides.checkboxLabel: Banana + pan sauce + milk into blender

items: Scrape the banana and all the caramel sauce from the pan into a blender.
Add ¼ cup milk.
Blend until smooth.

body.html: Don't leave any sauce in the pan. That's where most of the caramel flavor lives. Use a silicone spatula to get every drop.

slides.title: Add Ice Cream + Blend
slides.checkboxLabel: Add ice cream, blend to milkshake texture

items: Add ½ cup vanilla ice cream to the blender.
Blend in short pulses until thick and smooth — spoonable, not pourable.

body.html: Texture target: Holds a soft peak when you lift the blender lid. Too thin? More ice cream. Too thick? Splash more milk.

slides.title: Top + Serve
slides.checkboxLabel: Whipped cream, caramel, torch the sugar

items: Pour the milkshake into a tall glass.
Crown with whipped cream.
Drizzle caramel sauce over the top.
Optional: sprinkle a pinch of brown sugar on top and hit it with a kitchen torch until it bubbles and crackles.

body.html: Torch tip: Move the flame constantly — don't park it in one spot or the sugar burns black instead of bruleeing. A few seconds is enough.
